title: Recruitment Specialist Job at Maywood Lending Limited
content:
Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Recruitment Specialist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Recruitment Specialist to support our human resources operations.
	The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience across core HR functions, managing the entire recruitment process, from job posting to onboarding, ensuring a smooth hiring experience for both candidates and the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Developing and implementing recruitment strategies and procedures in alignment with the organization's objectives.
	Working closely with hiring managers to define job requirements, roles, and responsibilities.
	Writing and posting job descriptions on various platforms to attract qualified candidates.
	Screening and shortlisting candidates based on established criteria.
	Conducting initial interviews and coordinating follow-up interviews with team leaders or direct supervisors.
	Managing the job offer process, including negotiating employment terms and finalizing offers.
	Organizing and participating in job fairs and recruitment events to build a strong employer brand.
	Keeping up-to-date with the latest trends in recruitment and employment practices.
	Maintaining a database of potential candidates for future job openings.
	Ensuring compliance with all federal, state, and local regulations regarding employment.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 3 years experience in a generalist HR role
	Strong interpersonal and communication skills
	Professional certification in Human Resources is a plus.
	Proven experience in recruitment or a similar role, with a track record of successful hiring campaigns.
	Proficiency in using HR software and candidate management systems.
	Ability to work independently as well as part of a team.
	Good understanding of Nigerian labor laws and HR practices
	High level of organization and attention to detail


Method of Application 
Interested candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using “Recruitment Specialist” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We&#39;re looking for a detail-oriented finance officer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will handle financial transactions, manage accounts, and ensure compliance with tax regulations.


Responsibilities


	Manage financial records and accounts
	Handle personal income tax and withholding tax (WHT) compliance
	Process payments and invoices
	Reconcile accounts and prepare financial reports
	Assist in budgeting and forecasting
	Work with ERP software (QuickBooks or similar) .


Requirements


	BSc Degree in Accounting / Finance or related fields
	3+ years of experience in accounting/finance
	Knowledge of Nigerian tax laws (PIT, WHT)
	Proficient in QuickBooks or similar ERP software
	Strong analytical and organizational skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to&nbsp;idowu@maywood.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Finance Officer Job at Maywood Lending Limited




        
    

        
        Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We're looking for a detail-oriented finance officer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will handle financial transactions, manage accounts, and ensure compliance with tax regulations.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage financial records and accounts
	Handle personal income tax and withholding tax (WHT) compliance
	Process payments and invoices
	Reconcile accounts and prepare financial reports
	Assist in budgeting and forecasting
	Work with ERP software (QuickBooks or similar) .


Requirements


	BSc Degree in Accounting / Finance or related fields
	3+ years of experience in accounting/finance
	Knowledge of Nigerian tax laws (PIT, WHT)
	Proficient in QuickBooks or similar ERP software
	Strong analytical and organizational skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Posiiton: Human Resources Officer&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Agidingbi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assist in end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, shortlisting, interviews, onboarding).
	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases accurately.
	Support the administration of employee benefits, payroll inputs, and leave management.
	Help implement HR policies and procedures to ensure compliance with labor laws and company standards.
	Provide support in performance management activities, including appraisals and feedback processes.
	Address routine employee queries regarding HR policies, benefits, and procedures.
	Assist in organizing training sessions, employee engagement activities, and other HR-related events.
	Prepare HR-related reports as required (headcount, turnover, leave summaries, etc.).
	Support disciplinary and grievance processes under supervision.
	Perform other HR administrative duties as assigned.


&nbsp;Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	3 - 5 years of relevant experience in HR.
	Knowledge of HR best practices and Nigerian labor law (or relevant local labor laws).
	Good interpersonal and communication skills.
	Strong organizational and administrative abilities.
	Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint); familiarity with HRIS is an advantage.
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information professionally.


Salary
N150,000 - N180,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;idowu@maywood.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Human Resources Officer Job at Maywood Lending Limited




        
    

        
        Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Posiiton: Human Resources Officer 

Job Location: Agidingbi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, shortlisting, interviews, onboarding).
	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases accurately.
	Support the administration of employee benefits, payroll inputs, and leave management.
	Help implement HR policies and procedures to ensure compliance with labor laws and company standards.
	Provide support in performance management activities, including appraisals and feedback processes.
	Address routine employee queries regarding HR policies, benefits, and procedures.
	Assist in organizing training sessions, employee engagement activities, and other HR-related events.
	Prepare HR-related reports as required (headcount, turnover, leave summaries, etc.).
	Support disciplinary and grievance processes under supervision.
	Perform other HR administrative duties as assigned.


 Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or a related field.
	3 - 5 years of relevant experience in HR.
	Knowledge of HR best practices and Nigerian labor law (or relevant local labor laws).
	Good interpersonal and communication skills.
	Strong organizational and administrative abilities.
	Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint); familiarity with HRIS is an advantage.
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information professionally.


Salary
N150,000 - N180,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Chief Legal Officer (CLO)",
  "description": "Safritecture&nbsp;- At the intersection of Culture, Tradition, Science, Art, Technology, Technology(z), and (spi)rituality sits Safritecture. It is here that we create and realise Afro-conscious designs with depth and meaning. This is our DNA. We are a pan-African design, build and branding start-up. Our mission is to expand our activities and become truly pan-African. Our Command Centre will be in Lagos, Nigeria, and Country Offices are provisioned for the 53 other African sovereign states.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Legal Officer (CLO)

Job Location:&nbsp;Command Centre, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting to: The Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
Contract Type: Annual&nbsp;
Working Hours:&nbsp;8am to 6pm. Monday &ndash; Friday. Flexible hours policy
Tools:&nbsp;Company car, new laptop.
Perquisites:&nbsp;To be stated in the Offer Letter

Description


	Safritecture is looking for an experienced individual to act as its Chief Legal Officer (CLO), to take care of our day-to-day legal operations.
	Our audacious plan is to establish viable Safritecture offices in the capital cities of each sovereign African state between Q2 2026 and Q1 2027.
	Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), you will be expected to provide full legal support and documentation for the entire organization.
	The ability to problem solve, use your own judgement, think creatively, and work calmly under pressure are all essential skills to the role.
	Participation in the monthly Command Centre Meetings and policy formation in line with the Safritecture Business Model is mandatory, as you are expected to anchor the legal presentations.
	Registration with the necessary legal governing bodies in Nigeria is compulsory.


Roles and Responsibilities


	The primary responsibility of a CLO is to oversee the legal department and ensure that the organization is operating within the bounds of the law. This includes interpreting laws, rules, and regulations that are relevant to the company&rsquo;s operations.
	A Safritecture CLO does much more than this, and being an exceptional legal talent is the non-negotiable starting point.
	Ideally, you should also be a strategic thinker and a senior executive. Operating at the highest and most impactful level within Safritecture, you should have the ability (or at least the strong desire to acquire the ability) to execute the four following roles:


Strategist:


	As a strategist, you are in the position to bring a clear legal strategy that supports and enables business strategy.
	In association with the Head of Communications (HOC) and the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) you are to provide the CEO and the Board with legal guidance that enables the achievement of Safritecture &rsquo;s growth objectives.


Responsibilities include:


	Building strong relationships with Safritecture leadership teams.
	Building strong relationships with board members.
	Understanding the strategy and value creation levers for the business, and reflecting them in our legal strategy.
	Serving as a trusted business adviser who proactively engages management and the board around strategic business issues.
	Anticipating and proactively developing a strategy to address changes in regulatory requirements and public policy.


Catalyst:


	&nbsp;A catalyst enables competitive advantage by bringing a legal lens and the critical guidance needed to facilitate the CEO&rsquo;s ability to select the right path forward and set the tone at the top for Safritecture.


Responsibilities include:


	Assessing whether business is carried out in the right way, developing the corporate risk profile, and a strong companywide compliance framework.
	Empowering the legal team to serve as trusted business advisers who are sought out to proactively help Safritecture&nbsp;develop solutions.
	Taking the lead in building a strong corporate culture that is aligned with the values of Safritecture around compliance and integrity.
	Challenging the status quo as a change agent.
	Driving satisfaction and experience of Safritecture clients, with legal as a value-added service


Guardian:


	In Guardian mode, you are expected to guide legal and regulatory matters for Safritecture, navigating complexity while mitigating risk.
	This is the foundational responsibility for the CLO and the in-house legal team.


Responsibilities include:


	Guarding corporate integrity and tone at the top within the Legal Unit.
	Understanding business and compliance risk, especially as it relates to drafting contracts with multiple vendors across Africa.
	Maintaining clarity between the roles of the board and management.
	Identifying and remediating exposure to brand and reputation risk.
	Managing governance approach on regulatory and legal issues.


Operator:


	As an operator, you will spend much of your time focusing on how to optimize the capabilities, talent, service levels, costs, and legal structure required to best serve Safritecture.


Responsibilities include:


	Creating leadership development and succession plans for the Legal Unit.
	Developing in conjunction with the Head of Communications (HOC) and the CEO, and implementing key performance metrics for the Legal Unit and its external counsel.
	Identifying skill and subject matter expertise gaps, and developing plans to fill them; identifying and implementing technology solutions for the Legal Unit.
	Refining the legal operating model to increase the effectiveness of the Legal Unit.
	Developing in conjunction with the Chief Financial Officer (CFO), accurate forecasting and reporting of legal spend.


Skills Set:


	Display a high capacity for multitasking.
	Maintain an intense level of professionalism while handling confidential information.
	Ability to communicate clearly and frequently.
	Effective use of Microsoft Office, especially Excel.
	Deep knowledge of financial accounting, payroll, and inventory software.


Language Skills:&nbsp;


	Proficiency in the English language is preferred.


Remuneration
$30,000 / annum minimum, excluding bonuses &amp; allowances.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified firms should send their CV and a short summary of how you envisage a viable working relationship with Safritecture to:&nbsp;safritecture@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;Please ensure your CV states your date of birth and the dates of your qualifications.",

Chief Legal Officer (CLO) Job at Safritecture




        
    

        
        Safritecture - At the intersection of Culture, Tradition, Science, Art, Technology, Technology(z), and (spi)rituality sits Safritecture. It is here that we create and realise Afro-conscious designs with depth and meaning. This is our DNA. We are a pan-African design, build and branding start-up. Our mission is to expand our activities and become truly pan-African. Our Command Centre will be in Lagos, Nigeria, and Country Offices are provisioned for the 53 other African sovereign states.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Legal Officer (CLO)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Command Centre, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting to: The Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
Contract Type: Annual 
Working Hours: 8am to 6pm. Monday – Friday. Flexible hours policy
Tools: Company car, new laptop.
Perquisites: To be stated in the Offer Letter

Description


	Safritecture is looking for an experienced individual to act as its Chief Legal Officer (CLO), to take care of our day-to-day legal operations.
	Our audacious plan is to establish viable Safritecture offices in the capital cities of each sovereign African state between Q2 2026 and Q1 2027.
	Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), you will be expected to provide full legal support and documentation for the entire organization.
	The ability to problem solve, use your own judgement, think creatively, and work calmly under pressure are all essential skills to the role.
	Participation in the monthly Command Centre Meetings and policy formation in line with the Safritecture Business Model is mandatory, as you are expected to anchor the legal presentations.
	Registration with the necessary legal governing bodies in Nigeria is compulsory.


Roles and Responsibilities


	The primary responsibility of a CLO is to oversee the legal department and ensure that the organization is operating within the bounds of the law. This includes interpreting laws, rules, and regulations that are relevant to the company’s operations.
	A Safritecture CLO does much more than this, and being an exceptional legal talent is the non-negotiable starting point.
	Ideally, you should also be a strategic thinker and a senior executive. Operating at the highest and most impactful level within Safritecture, you should have the ability (or at least the strong desire to acquire the ability) to execute the four following roles:


Strategist:


	As a strategist, you are in the position to bring a clear legal strategy that supports and enables business strategy.
	In association with the Head of Communications (HOC) and the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) you are to provide the CEO and the Board with legal guidance that enables the achievement of Safritecture ’s growth objectives.


Responsibilities include:


	Building strong relationships with Safritecture leadership teams.
	Building strong relationships with board members.
	Understanding the strategy and value creation levers for the business, and reflecting them in our legal strategy.
	Serving as a trusted business adviser who proactively engages management and the board around strategic business issues.
	Anticipating and proactively developing a strategy to address changes in regulatory requirements and public policy.


Catalyst:


	 A catalyst enables competitive advantage by bringing a legal lens and the critical guidance needed to facilitate the CEO’s ability to select the right path forward and set the tone at the top for Safritecture.


Responsibilities include:


	Assessing whether business is carried out in the right way, developing the corporate risk profile, and a strong companywide compliance framework.
	Empowering the legal team to serve as trusted business advisers who are sought out to proactively help Safritecture develop solutions.
	Taking the lead in building a strong corporate culture that is aligned with the values of Safritecture around compliance and integrity.
	Challenging the status quo as a change agent.
	Driving satisfaction and experience of Safritecture clients, with legal as a value-added service


Guardian:


	In Guardian mode, you are expected to guide legal and regulatory matters for Safritecture, navigating complexity while mitigating risk.
	This is the foundational responsibility for the CLO and the in-house legal team.


Responsibilities include:


	Guarding corporate integrity and tone at the top within the Legal Unit.
	Understanding business and compliance risk, especially as it relates to drafting contracts with multiple vendors across Africa.
	Maintaining clarity between the roles of the board and management.
	Identifying and remediating exposure to brand and reputation risk.
	Managing governance approach on regulatory and legal issues.


Operator:


	As an operator, you will spend much of your time focusing on how to optimize the capabilities, talent, service levels, costs, and legal structure required to best serve Safritecture.


Responsibilities include:


	Creating leadership development and succession plans for the Legal Unit.
	Developing in conjunction with the Head of Communications (HOC) and the CEO, and implementing key performance metrics for the Legal Unit and its external counsel.
	Identifying skill and subject matter expertise gaps, and developing plans to fill them; identifying and implementing technology solutions for the Legal Unit.
	Refining the legal operating model to increase the effectiveness of the Legal Unit.
	Developing in conjunction with the Chief Financial Officer (CFO), accurate forecasting and reporting of legal spend.


Skills Set:


	Display a high capacity for multitasking.
	Maintain an intense level of professionalism while handling confidential information.
	Ability to communicate clearly and frequently.
	Effective use of Microsoft Office, especially Excel.
	Deep knowledge of financial accounting, payroll, and inventory software.


Language Skills: 


	Proficiency in the English language is preferred.


Remuneration
$30,000 / annum minimum, excluding bonuses & allowances.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified firms should send their CV and a short summary of how you envisage a viable working relationship with Safritecture to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Please ensure your CV states your date of birth and the dates of your qualifications.

  "description": "Chrisvirgy Homes is a renowned multi-disciplinary property service firm, that is privately owned and incorporated in Nigeria.&nbsp;It is a rapidly expanding firm that offers its client&rsquo;s customized solutions across a wide variety of real estate services.&nbsp;Our success has been built on the fact that we do not require money from our clients to complete or expedite any of our products.&nbsp;We have a wonderful working relationship with our clients because of our credibility and we met their real estate needs with creativity and passion.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a smart, organized, and professional Administrative Assistant to support daily operations in our apartment. The ideal candidate will assist management, coordinate administrative tasks, and ensure smooth communication between departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide administrative support to hotel management
	Handle emails, phone calls, and internal communication
	Maintain guest records and office files
	Assist with staff scheduling and coordination
	Prepare reports, documents, and daily summaries
	Manage office supplies and inventory
	Support front desk operations when needed
	Coordinate with departments (Housekeeping, Front Desk, Maintenance)


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND/HND/Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration or related field with&nbsp;3-5 years of experience in an administrative role (hotel experience is an advantage)
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Attention to detail and professionalism


Preferred Qualities:


	Friendly and customer-focused attitude
	Ability to work in a fast-paced hotel environment
	Good problem-solving skills
	Professional appearance and conduct


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;chrisvirgyreservations@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Administrative Assistant Job at Chrisvirgy Homes




        
    

        
        Chrisvirgy Homes is a renowned multi-disciplinary property service firm, that is privately owned and incorporated in Nigeria. It is a rapidly expanding firm that offers its client’s customized solutions across a wide variety of real estate services. Our success has been built on the fact that we do not require money from our clients to complete or expedite any of our products. We have a wonderful working relationship with our clients because of our credibility and we met their real estate needs with creativity and passion.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Assistant

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	We are looking for a smart, organized, and professional Administrative Assistant to support daily operations in our apartment. The ideal candidate will assist management, coordinate administrative tasks, and ensure smooth communication between departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide administrative support to hotel management
	Handle emails, phone calls, and internal communication
	Maintain guest records and office files
	Assist with staff scheduling and coordination
	Prepare reports, documents, and daily summaries
	Manage office supplies and inventory
	Support front desk operations when needed
	Coordinate with departments (Housekeeping, Front Desk, Maintenance)


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND/HND/Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration or related field with 3-5 years of experience in an administrative role (hotel experience is an advantage)
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Attention to detail and professionalism


Preferred Qualities:


	Friendly and customer-focused attitude
	Ability to work in a fast-paced hotel environment
	Good problem-solving skills
	Professional appearance and conduct


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
